title: Bernard Arnault, generous patron of the arts and lover of tax breaks
subhead: 'Bernard Arnault's Empire' (5/6). France's richest man is also one of Europe's leading art collectors. Through the Fondation Louis Vuitton, he combines his passion for the arts with highly lucrative tax arrangements.

title: European Commission fines Google €890 million, risking Trump's ire

In the West Bank, a Palestinian hamlet faces relentless harassment by settlers
subhead: In the Bedouin village of Umm Al-Khair, one of the communities in the southern West Bank surrounded by Israeli settlements and military bases, the Al-Hathaleen family must endure the daily violence of their 'neighbors.'

title: Fields medal awarded to four young mathematicians
subhead: Recognized as the Nobel Prize in mathematics, the Fields medal honors researchers under the age of 40 for outstanding discoveries. Of the four recipients, one winner, 35-year-old Hong Wang, is only the third woman to receive the honor since its inception in 1936.

title: Chanel's XXL cruise ship in May 2018
subhead: 'Behind the scene' (2/6). Fashion houses often use runway shows as opportunities for particularly spectacular set designs. In May 2018, Karl Lagerfeld docked a life-size cruise ship under the glass dome of the Grand Palais in Paris for Chanel's cruise collection show.

title: The mysterious horse-shaped geoglyph England has been maintaining for thousands of years
subhead: The Uffington White Horse is one of the best known geoglyphs in the UK. Drawn around 3,000 years ago on a hillside in Oxfordshire, its chalk outline is maintained each year by volunteers through a restoration campaign.

How Macron narrowly averted his environment minister's resignation
subhead: Monique Barbut, who said she intended to quit the government on Tuesday, July 21, after an agricultural law she contested was adopted, ended up staying on in her role on Wednesday. The twist added a colorful note to the political crisis.

title: My life as a Russian exile in Paris: 'You can't fight French bureaucracy'
subhead: 'A Russian in Paris' (1/6). Journalist Elizaveta Osetinskaya, who came to France in 2022 because of her opposition to the war in Ukraine, describes the difficulties she has faced with the prefecture while barely speaking the language.

title: How Bernard Arnault works to keep his media outlets in line
subhead: 'Bernard Arnault's Empire' (4/6). Since the 1990s, the CEO of LVMH has built a small media galaxy in print and radio, which he uses to convey his ideas and tastes, but also to promote his brands.
